name: Double Eagle Pawn Shop
description_short: Family-owned pawn shop in Douglasville, GA offering collateral-based loans on firearms, jewelry, electronics, and ammunition with extended grace periods up to 30 days.
description_long: Double Eagle Pawn Shop is a family-owned and operated pawnbroker located in Douglasville, Georgia, founded in 1987. The business has been serving the local community for over 30 years with traditional pawnbroking services. The owner is an active member of the Georgia National Guard and the shop emphasizes community involvement and military service. As a pawn shop, Double Eagle functions as a traditional collateral-based lender, allowing customers to pledge items of value in exchange for immediate cash loans. The business model operates on the principle that pawnbroking represents the oldest form of credit and positions itself as a local lending alternative. Double Eagle carries a diverse inventory across multiple product categories. Their firearms selection includes hunting rifles, sporting firearms, target weapons, and antique pieces. The shop stocks ammunition for customers looking to purchase supplies. Electronics inventory ranges from televisions to tablets and other consumer devices. Their jewelry department features a large selection described as suitable for gift-giving occasions. The company distinguishes itself through several specific policies and community commitments. Most notably, they extend loan grace periods up to 30 days past the standard maturity date, demonstrating a customer-friendly approach to redemption flexibility. The business offers military and law enforcement discounts, reflecting the owner's background in the Georgia National Guard. Management emphasizes that they prioritize customer relationships over acquisition of merchandise, suggesting a service-oriented philosophy. Their stated mission is to be "your local lender" rather than simply a reseller of pawned goods. Double Eagle operates with limited hours—Monday through Friday from noon to 6 PM and Saturday from 10 AM to 2 PM, with no Sunday operations. This structure may create accessibility challenges for customers with traditional work schedules. While the extended grace period is customer-friendly, pawn loans typically carry implicit costs through the interest and fees structure, though specific rates are not disclosed on the website. The business serves a traditional niche market but operates within the broader context of alternative lending where transparency around total borrowing costs is limited.
pros: Extended 30-day grace period past loan maturity date—more time to reclaim items than typical pawn shops
Military and law enforcement discounts available, reflecting community values and owner's GA National Guard service
Diverse inventory across firearms, ammunition, electronics, jewelry, and antique items
Family-owned and operated for 30+ years, suggesting stability and local community presence
No-pressure customer philosophy: "We want your business not your stuff"
Established since 1987 with stated commitment to being the local lender
cons: Limited operating hours (closed Sundays, only open until 6 PM weekdays) may not serve customers with standard work schedules
No transparent pricing or APR information disclosed on website—borrowing costs remain unclear
Pawn loans inherently carry implicit costs and fees that can be expensive relative to traditional credit sources
Saturday hours limited to 10 AM-2 PM, restricting weekend accessibility
No online application, loan calculator, or digital loan management tools mentioned
best_for: Customers with firearms, jewelry, or electronics to pledge who need immediate cash and live in Douglasville, GA
Military members and law enforcement seeking collateral-based loans with available discounts
Individuals who own items they're willing to pledge temporarily and can reclaim within the extended 30-day grace period
Community members preferring local, family-owned businesses over corporate pawn chains
diagnosis: Double Eagle Pawn Shop is best for Douglasville-area residents who own valuable items (firearms, jewelry, electronics) and need immediate cash through collateral-based lending, particularly military members and law enforcement who qualify for discounts. The main caveat is that pawn loans carry implicit costs that are not transparently disclosed, and the limited operating hours may create accessibility barriers for working customers.
services: Collateral-based pawn loans on firearms, jewelry, electronics, and ammunition
30-day extended grace period past loan maturity date for item redemption
Hunting and sporting firearms sales and loans
Television and electronics loans and sales
Jewelry sales and loans for gift-giving purposes
Military and law enforcement discounts on pawn services
In-person pawnbroking services at Douglasville location
